attacking_style: Attacks from established breakaways on final climbs rather than initiating moves from the pelon. Strikes with 1-2 climbs remaining when already in a reduced front group.
team_tactics: Operates with limited team support, relying on breakaway cooperation rather than domestique-led setup. Positioned for stage hunting rather than GC protection.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages with early breakaway opportunities and decisive final climbs. Strong on steep gradients at altitude. Vulnerable on flat, technical finishes where positioning matters.
vulnerability: Positioning weakness on flat sprint stages leading to crash risk. Lacks team support for GC ambitions or controlling races. Requires breakaway formation to be effective.
key_patterns: Wins from established breakaways, not solo attacks from peloton (Giro stage win)
Climbing form peaks in Grand Tours after extended stage-hunting campaigns (Giro 2021)
Crash risk on flat technical stages suggests poor positioning skills outside mountains
Operates as stage hunter rather than GC contender despite climbing ability
form_trajectory: positive: Rediscovered climbing form with first European win after years of trying, maintaining form into altitude stages
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3
